summary_one_line: Input must contain an sCAL chunk whose 4-byte big-endian length field is < 4, hitting the short-chunk reject path.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A well-formed PNG carrying an sCAL chunk (chunk-type FOURCC 's','C','A','L') placed after IHDR and before IDAT, where the chunk's 4-byte big-endian length field holds a value strictly less than 4 so png_handle_sCAL takes the `length < 4` true branch at pngrutil.c:2403.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds (vpc/cmplog) reach line 2403 and take its true side (W: T=18 F=15) and the blocker becomes hit (avg hitcount on branch winner=18 / 34), meaning the fuzzer produced an sCAL chunk with a length field <4; the I2S winners place the correct chunk-type literal bytes at the chunk position so the sCAL handler is dispatched at all.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds never take the true side (L: T=0 F=25) and the branch hitcount stays 0; without I2S the loser fails to synthesize the exact 4-byte sCAL chunk-type tag (and a sub-4 length) by random byte mutation, so png_handle_sCAL with length<4 is never dispatched.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc carry I2SRandReplace, which reads the CmpLogObserver record of the chunk-name comparison and substitutes the logged 'sCAL' FOURCC operand at the matching input offset, manufacturing the chunk-type literal that random havoc bytes (naive/value_profile) cannot place; once dispatched, a small/absent length field satisfies length<4.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the true side of `else if (length < 4)` inside png_handle_sCAL, the globally blocked side.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
Source line:    else if (length < 4)
Globally blocked side: T  (true branch)

evidence_trail.claim: Reaching the blocker requires the sCAL chunk handler to be entered after IHDR and before IDAT.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: /* Need unit type, width, \0, height: minimum 4 bytes */

evidence_trail.claim: Winner inputs take the true side of L2403 while loser inputs never do.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L2403  T=18 F=15  T=0 F=25  else if (length < 4)  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Both decisive pairs carry the I2S technique delta and the winners resolve the branch while losers block.
The winner produces hits on the branch where the loser produces none.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=18  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog/vpc add I2SRandReplace, which substitutes a logged CMP operand at the matching input offset.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile / naive have no I2SRandReplace mutator, so they cannot perform the operand substitution.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 4031 --role W --fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --trial 1 --seed 384ddb337e2d2818` and observed I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depths 2 and 3; also ran it for cmplog seed 774ddf3ddc65ad44 (trial 1) and observed a dash row at depth 14 — both winning chains for the two I2S pairs contain at least one I2S-floor ancestor.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating only on a length<4 integer comparison with NO preceding multi-byte literal chunk-type FOURCC match would show naive/value_profile resolving it as fast as cmplog/vpc (no I2S advantage), refuting that the divergence comes from I2S substitution of the chunk-type literal.

weakest_evidence_point: The seed byte dumps only show the first 0x40 bytes (PNG header/gAMA region), so I cannot directly point at the sCAL chunk-type tag or its sub-4 length field in the input — the FOURCC-substitution claim rests on the source dispatch structure plus the I2S-floor lineage rather than visible chunk bytes.
